skill.name: writing-skills
skill.description: Use when creating, editing, or testing AI skills before deployment.
skill.summary: Guides test-driven creation and verification of reusable AI skills.
skill.author: Dimon94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives from markdown examples, copied best-practice text, URLs, and terminology. The confirmed risk is an included Node utility that invokes Graphviz with child_process and writes generated files, so publication is reasonable with an execution warning.

medium_findings.title: Utility Script Executes Graphviz
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE with limited scope. render-graphs.js imports child_process and runs dot -Tsvg plus which dot. The command strings are fixed, so command injection evidence was not found, but executing a local binary on skill-supplied DOT content is an elevated capability.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The executable behavior is explicit and confirmed in the script. Confidence is below very high because the command is hardcoded and not built from untrusted shell text.

medium_findings.title: Utility Script Writes Generated Files
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE with legitimate purpose. The script resolves a user-provided skill directory, reads SKILL.md, creates a diagrams directory, and writes SVG and DOT outputs. This is expected behavior for a rendering helper, but users should understand it modifies the target skill directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The read and write calls are direct and confirmed. Risk is moderate because writes are constrained to a diagrams subdirectory under the selected skill directory.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Trigger Static Rules
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for most static hits. The repeated external command, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and C2 keyword matches occur in markdown examples, image URLs, file naming examples, and explanatory text rather than executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Sampled static locations show documentation and fenced examples, not runtime behavior. The large duplicated hit count is consistent with markdown syntax and terminology false positives.

low_findings.title: Prompt-Like Testing Scenarios Are Included
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW but low risk. The skill includes pressure-test prompts such as real-scenario instructions and forced choices for evaluating agent compliance. These are framed as test material, not instructions to override marketplace review or system policy.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text has prompt-like imperatives, but context shows a legitimate testing reference. No evidence found of instructions to skip security analysis or lower risk ratings.

dangerous_patterns.title: child_process.execSync Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses execSync to call Graphviz. This should be disclosed because running the helper executes local commands, even though no command injection vector was confirmed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is direct and executable. The impact is moderated by fixed command strings and stdin input rather than shell-concatenated user input.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Diagram File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The script creates a diagrams directory and writes SVG or DOT files. This is useful, but it is still filesystem modification triggered by running the skill helper.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file creation behavior is explicit. The finding is not high confidence malicious because the paths are deterministic outputs under the selected skill directory.

content.value_statement: Skill authors need a repeatable way to write instructions that agents will discover and follow. This skill applies test-driven documentation, pressure scenarios, and concise authoring rules to improve Claude, Codex, and Claude Code skills.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to structure SKILL.md frontmatter, descriptions, and supporting files.
Applies red-green-refactor thinking to skill documentation and verification.
Provides pressure-test formats for checking whether agents follow a skill under constraints.
Includes Anthropic skill authoring guidance as a local reference file.
Documents Graphviz conventions for small process flowcharts.
Includes a Node utility for rendering DOT blocks from SKILL.md into SVG diagrams.
limitations: The skill does not automatically create or install marketplace packages.
Human judgment is still required to decide whether pressure-test results are acceptable.
The rendering helper requires Node.js, Graphviz, and permission to execute local commands.
Directory examples mention Claude and Codex defaults that may differ by environment.

use_cases.title: Write a New Skill
use_cases.description: Create a concise skill with clear trigger conditions, useful supporting files, and testable behavior.

use_cases.title: Improve Skill Discovery
use_cases.description: Rewrite names, descriptions, and keywords so Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can find the skill at the right time.

use_cases.title: Verify Agent Compliance
use_cases.description: Design pressure scenarios and subagent tests that reveal whether a skill changes behavior under realistic constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new skill for [task]. Start with the triggering conditions, then draft a concise SKILL.md outline with required frontmatter.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Discovery Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill description for discovery quality. Make it start with Use when, remove workflow summaries, and add searchable symptoms.

prompt_templates.title: Design Pressure Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three pressure scenarios that test whether an agent follows this skill when speed, sunk cost, or authority pressure is present.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor After Failed Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these failed pressure-test transcripts, identify the rationalizations, update the skill to close each loophole, and define re-test criteria.

output_examples.input: A vague skill description that explains the whole workflow.
output_examples.output: A shorter trigger-focused description, a list of searchable symptoms, and notes about what moved into the body.

output_examples.input: A discipline skill that agents keep bypassing under deadline pressure.
output_examples.output: Pressure scenarios, observed rationalizations, explicit counters, and a re-test checklist.

output_examples.input: A skill with several decision points and confusing prose.
output_examples.output: A simplified structure with one small flowchart, clearer headings, and supporting files only where needed.

best_practices: Run a baseline pressure test before writing or changing the skill.
Keep the description focused on when to use the skill, not how the workflow works.
Move heavy references or reusable tools into separate files with clear access instructions.
anti_patterns: Writing a skill from intuition without first observing agent failure.
Using the description field as a compressed workflow that agents may follow instead of reading the skill.
Adding long examples, many languages, or decorative flowcharts that increase context cost without improving behavior.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It helps authors create skills that agents can discover, read, and follow under realistic pressure.

faq.question: Does it create skills automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides authoring and testing guidance. The user still writes, edits, and verifies the final files.

faq.question: Why does it use test-driven development terms?
faq.answer: It maps red, green, and refactor phases to documentation testing so behavior changes can be verified.

faq.question: Is the included rendering script required?
faq.answer: No. The script is optional and only helps render Graphviz flowcharts from skill markdown.

faq.question: Can this be used with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance discusses both Claude and Codex skill directories, but local setup may vary.

faq.question: What should reviewers watch before publishing?
faq.answer: Reviewers should disclose the optional script execution and verify that pressure-test prompts are used only as examples.
